description: Narrative event/state profile for fictional or real-world scene ingestion: actors, objects, places, possession, movement, observation, use, damage, and simple corrections.

use_when: object interactions such as tasted, ate, sat, lay, broke, entered, exited
who did what to which object

semantic_ir_context: profile_scope: story-world semantic ingestion; capture direct narrative events and states without turning a story into a summary
identity_policy: preserve named actors and stable object identity across turns; ask or quarantine when a pronoun has multiple plausible referents

source_fidelity_policy: use text-local names for durable story facts; do not replace Little Wee Bear/Middle-sized Bear/Great Big Bear with Baby Bear/Mama Bear/Papa Bear unless the utterance or context explicitly establishes those aliases
alias_policy: plausible story-canon aliases belong in self_check notes or unsafe_implications, not in committed candidate_operation arguments, until grounded by source text or KB context
unknown_actor_policy: do not commit unknown_agent writes; prefer passive object-state predicates such as was_tasted/1, was_eaten/1, was_sat_in/1, or was_lain_in/1 when available

event_policy: use specific predicates for movement, tasting, eating, sitting, lying, sleeping, seeing, finding, entering, exiting, returning from an activity, and breaking; do not use inside/2 or carries/2 as generic fallbacks
trait_policy: use has_trait/2 for descriptions such as hair color, size, voice, or physical qualities; do not encode traits as owns/2
temporal_policy: preserve order in assertions/self_check when exact temporal predicates are unavailable; do not invent timestamps

canonical_palette.notes: Durable possession or belonging.
Location when no more specific movement predicate applies.
Physical containment only; not a generic relation.
Boundary crossing into a place.
Boundary crossing out of a place.
Return from an activity or location.
Discovery of an object or place.
Descriptive trait such as hair color, size, voice, or physical quality; not possession.
Robot or artificial unit identity when explicit.
Direct tasting event.
Eating event.
Complete consumption event.
Sitting in/on a chair or seat.
Lying in/on a bed.
Sleeping location.
Object became broken.
Passive observed tasting when actor is unknown.
Passive observed eating/consumption when actor is unknown.
Passive observed seat use when actor is unknown.
Passive observed bed use when actor is unknown.
